title: GitLab Secret Detection Scanner Block Events on Default Branch
dataSources: GitLab Secret Detection reports ingested as `gitlab:pipeline` job output or dedicated `gitlab:secret_detection` HEC events
app: GitLab Ultimate Secret Detection + webhook; or pipeline artifact parsed to Splunk
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline" OR sourcetype="gitlab:secret_detection"
| search (scanner="secret_detection" OR "Secret Detection" OR secret_detection) AND (blocked=1 OR status="failed")
| eval ref_norm=lower(ref)
| where ref_norm IN ("main","master","default","production")
| stats latest(_time) as last_block, values(ref) as branches by project_path, commit_sha
| sort -last_block
description: Hard blocks on default branch indicate exposed credentials that must rotate immediately.
value: Prioritizes secret leaks on trunk so incident response starts before lateral movement.
implementation: Map scanner job names; include `commit_sha` and MR IID when present. Open Sev-1 if default branch blocks spike.
visualization: Table (project, commit, branch), Timeline of blocks.
